justification: B writes the more sophisticated narrative — it adds Restauracja Chinska Shanghai as a declining-legacy reference, traces Monsun's lineage to Alga Street Food, quantifies review velocity (27/mo), and frames the Local-Guide share as a neighborhood-vs-transient proxy. Coverage and specificity tilt to B. But B's drawer shows only google.com (2 domains) while the prose name-drops Pyszne.pl 4.8/5, Uber Eats Gong Bao satisfaction, Wanderlog, and local food vlogs — sourcing claims the report cannot back. A actually pulls from six domains (eatzon, kukbuk, trojmiasto, wanderlog, wolt, google) and delivers equally concrete sentiment numbers (4.30 recent average, 16% Biang Biang mention rate, 34% Local Guides, 74/26 PL/EN split, 22% negative-response rate) without inventing citations. As the operator, A is the trustable read; B's hallucinated multi-platform sourcing is disqualifying on a sentiment-themes question. Specialist set is identical.
